solution:

v→=dr→dt=ω-sin⁡ωti^+cos⁡ωtj^

So v→⊥r→

subject: Physics
solution:
Range is same for complimentary angles. $\mathrm{T}_1=\frac{2 \mathrm{u} \sin \theta}{\mathrm{g}}$ and $\mathrm{T}_2=\frac{2 \mathrm{u} \sin (90-\theta)}{\mathrm{g}}$ and $R=\frac{u^2 \sin 2 \theta}{\mathrm{g}}$
$$
\therefore \mathrm{T}_1 \mathrm{~T}_2=\frac{2 \mathrm{u} \sin \theta}{\mathrm{g}} \times \frac{2 \mathrm{u} \cos \theta}{\mathrm{g}}=\frac{2 \mathrm{R}}{\mathrm{g}} \text {. }
$$

subject: Physics
solution:
The range R1 of the first projectile is given by

R1=u12sin2θ1g   ...(1)

The range R2 of the second projectile is given by

R2=u22sin2θ2g   ...(2)

Divide equation (1) by equation (2) and simplify to obtain the ratio of the ranges.

R1R2=u12sin2θ1gu22sin2θ2g= u12sin2θ1u22sin2θ2   ...3

Substitute the values of the known parameters into equation (3) to calculate the required ratio.

 R1R2=402×sin60°602×sin120°=49

subject: Physics
solution:
After 2 s the velocity is 20 m s-1

⇒vx2+vy2=v2=400

⇒u2cos245°+usin45°-gt2=u22+u2-202=400

⇒u22+u22+400-402u=400

 ⇒u2=402u⇒u=402 m s-1

Now the maximum height of the projectile is given by,

H=u2sin2θ2g⇒H=160020×2×2=20 m

subject: Physics
solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{H}_{\mathrm{Max}}=\frac{(\mathrm{u} \sin \theta)^2}{2 \mathrm{~g}} \\ & \frac{\left(\mathrm{H}_{\max }\right)_1}{\left(\mathrm{H}_{\max }\right)_2}=\frac{\mathrm{u}^2 \sin ^2(45-\alpha)}{\mathrm{u}^2 \sin ^2(45+\alpha)} \\ & =\frac{\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \cos \alpha-\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \sin \alpha\right)^2}{\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \cos \alpha+\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \sin \alpha\right)^2} \\ & =\frac{1-\sin 2 \alpha}{1+\sin 2 \alpha}\end{aligned}$

subject: Physics
solution:
The maximum horizontal range of a projectile is given by $R_{\text{max}} = \dfrac{u^2}{g}$.

Given $R_{\text{max}} = 6.4$ m and $g = 10$ m/s$^2$.

Substituting the values:

$6.4 = \dfrac{u^2}{10}$

$u^2 = 64$

$u = 8$ m/s.

Answer: $8$

subject: Physics
solution:
The angular speed ω of the child can be calculated as follows:

ω=2π3.14=2 rad s-1

The formula to calculate the centrifugal force on the child is given by

Fr=mω2r   ...(1)

Substitute the values of the known parameters into equation (1) to calculate the required centrifugal force.

Fr=5 kg×2 rad s-12×2 m=40 N

subject: Physics
solution:
$\vec{v}=K y \hat{i}+K x \hat{j}$
$\frac{d x}{d t}=K y, \quad \frac{d y}{d t}=K x$
$\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d y}{d t} \times \frac{d t}{d x}=\frac{K x}{K y}$
$y d y=x d x$
$y^2=x^2+c$

subject: Physics
solution:
maximum vertical height $=\frac{u^2}{2 g}=10 \mathrm{~m}$
Horizontal range of a projectile $=\frac{u^2 \sin 2 \theta}{\mathrm{g}}$
Range is maximum when $\theta=45^{\circ}$
Maximum horizontal range $=\frac{\mathrm{u}^2}{\mathrm{~g}}$
Hence maximum horizontal distance $=20 \mathrm{~m}$.

subject: Physics
solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{k}_{\mathrm{i}}=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{mv}^2 \\ & \mathrm{k}_{\mathrm{f}}=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{~m}\left(\mathrm{v} \cos 60^{\circ}\right)^2=\frac{1}{8} m v^2 \\ & \Delta \mathrm{k}=\mathrm{k}_{\mathrm{i}}-\mathrm{k}_{\mathrm{f}}=\frac{3}{8} \mathrm{mv}^2=\frac{3}{8} \times 0.1 \times 400=15 \mathrm{~J}\end{aligned}$
